mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals acute labor market deterioration that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of accommodative monetary policy reflects capacity utilization expansion without inflationary pressure, supporting equity valuations and positive forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or Rank IC drops below zero in low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate inflation hedging behavior and equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPI growth is driven solely by energy components.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that compresses wage growth expectations, reducing corporate margin pressure and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when PAYEMS growth exceeds 300k monthly, or forward returns turn negative during labor supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP consistently forecasts corporate revenue expansion, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows, or GDP growth fails to predict equity returns during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during liquidity crises signal severe supply chain disruptions that force immediate equity deleveraging, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when VIX remains below 20, or forward returns are positive in over 50% of industrial production contraction months.
